content.question: Whether the Polish 1999 delayed-tracking reform's effect sizes (0.25–0.28 SD) would replicate in Hungary given different demographic composition (Roma representation, migration patterns) and school-system institutional differences.
content.reduction_path: Natural experiments or quasi-experiments within Hungary (e.g., regional pilot phase-outs of 8-year grammar schools with random assignment or propensity-score matching); administrative data on prior achievement, family SES, and post-selection outcomes for Poland and Hungary cohorts.
